codeReview.summary: Implements SessionService with full CRUD operations and state machine. All four VAL assertions (001, 002, 004, 005) are covered. Code is well-structured with 26 tests passing. One minor performance concern: get_session_with_messages() does not use explicit eager loading for messages, relying on lazy loading instead.

issues.description: get_session_with_messages() uses simple query without joinedload for messages. While lazy loading works (due to relationship definition in model), explicit eager loading would be more efficient and prevent N+1 query issues when accessing session.messages in the router.
issues.suggestedFix: Use joinedload: from sqlalchemy.orm import joinedload; db.query(SessionModel).options(joinedload(SessionModel.messages)).filter(...).first()

issues.description: delete_session() relies on cascade delete for messages. This works correctly with SQLite but cascade delete behavior can vary across database backends. Currently documented by the model's cascade='all, delete-orphan'.
issues.suggestedFix: No immediate fix needed. Consider explicit message deletion for cross-backend compatibility if other databases will be used.

summary: Session CRUD operations implementation is solid. All assertions verified via 26 passing tests. State machine correctly transitions intro->active->concluded. Session history and detail endpoints work as expected. Minor optimization opportunity around eager loading messages in get_session_with_messages().
